title: Hoe bladwijzers te herstellen, die verdwenen zijn na de Firefox update van 20 mei 2026
content:
Bladwijzers zijn verdwenen na de Firefox update van 20 mei 2026. Vanaf die datum is de PC gebruikt, waarbij nieuwe bladwijzers zijn aangemaakt.

Ik heb 15 kopieën gevonden in: C:Users\Hans\AppData\Roaming\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\1ruz2hob.default-release, waarvan een met datum 20-05-2026 (andere 14 zijn van eerdere datum). De kopieën vanaf 21-05-2026 staan in 8pekqop5.default.

Mijn vraag: Hoe kan ik de bladwijzers versie) van 20-05-2026 (die nu dus verdwenen zijn in mijn huidige versie) TOEVOEGEN aan de huidige versie (30-05-2026) ?

De procedure die ik zou kunnen volgen: 1. Exporteer de huidige Bookmarks naar een HTML bestand en plaats die op Bureaublad 2. Restore de versie van 20-05-2026 (waarbij mijn nieuwe bladwijzers worden overschreven) 3. Importeer Bookmarks van HTML (op Bureaublad) , waarbij hopelijk de bladwijzers worden TOEGEVOEGD en weer beschikbaar zullen zijn.

Is deze procedure correct en wat zijn de risico's ? Bij voorbaat dank voor een antwoord en een bruikbare oplossing

title: Failure To Display Pop Up
content: Hello, https://www.so.energy/myaccount/usage shows a vertical bar chart representing electricity usage over time. If I hover the mouse pointer over one of the bars in the Edge browser I see a pop up that gives details of the electricity use represented by the bar. If I do the same in Firefox the bar changes colour to indicate it has been selected but there is no pop up. Is there likely to be a Firefox setting that is preventing the pop ups from showing? Alan.
creator: Alan37

title: .tvpi no longer associated
content: I run Win10, and Firefox recently updated. I use https://partners.titantv.com/default.aspx as a TV guide for my Hauppauge TV tuner card. When I click on a program I want the PC to record, I click the red dot, which creates a program.tvpi file in my Downloads folder (see image attached). Up until recently, this type of file was associated so that clicking the red dot would automatically put it into the Hauppauge recording queue. However, the latest Firefox upgrade took that association away. So, now I have to double-click the program.tvpi file in the Downloads folder to activate it. Is there a way to re-associate it? I tried right-clicking the red dot, but there's no choice. I didn't see it in the Firefox, Settings, General, Applications feature. Thank you for the help.

title: Firefox on macOS loads, opening all windows open at last Quit; then closes all windows; fails to display the window selected from menu
content:
MacOS Sequoia 15.7.7; Firefox 141.0.2

The "one sentence" in the heading states the problem. I am unable to use Firefox.

Firefox loads. It opens windows (I had fourteen open when I last used it successfully; I have many, many tabs). Then it removes each window from the screen. Firefox is in the Menu bar, but no windows are displayed. The windows still show when selecting Window in the menu bar. Selecting a window has no visible effect. File>New Window opens a new window, which works as expected. With one new window open, File>New Window does not open another.

Typing a few characters in the Address Bar opens a dropdown with a list of tabs, each with the affordance, Switch to Tab. Clicking on one takes down the list but does not open a window.

I would like, for a start, instructions on how to use Firefox starting afresh with no windows. First: having set aside the data that causes this problem. I suppose that might be simply a new profile.
creator: joaquin.oakland

title: Firefox Profiles is actively destructive!
content: Today I got on my laptop and opened Firefox like I do every day. The browser looked different. It was an ugly purple color. A window showed me information about 'Profiles'. I didn't want any 'Profiles', I just wanted my regular Firefox Browser. So I deleted the Profiles. This also ended up deleting all of my bookmarks and passwords!! It took the last two hours and working with AI to recover my most recent backup which was two years old. Firefox hasn't been backing up any of my data that I actually need, and it actively deleted the things I rely on. Your devs and designers really messed up on this one. I bet no one uses Profiles, but everyone uses passwords and bookmarks to some degree. Why would you build it like that?! Shame, Mozilla. This was a serious error on your part. Bad product design. Make it easier to back up the things we need. Get rid of this useless 'Profiles' function that I bet no one uses.
creator: kevinjhawley
